purpose: Define representative user personas, workload acceptance rows, failure taxonomy, diagnostics, and negative claim tests for L0 interpose and future standalone replacement planning.

personas.name: Shell and coreutils operator
personas.goal: Run ordinary interactive and scripted Unix commands under L0/L1 without surprising stdout, stderr, exit status, or diagnostics.
personas.success_condition: Pinned shell/coreutils commands pass strict and hardened mode pair checks, and unsupported standalone claims remain blocked.

personas.name: Build and package engineer
personas.goal: Compile, link, install, and package software while libc interposition preserves process, filesystem, stdio, allocator, and dynamic-linker behavior.
personas.success_condition: Build and package style commands have deterministic replay scripts, clear failure classes, and no L2/L3 claim without startup and host-dependency evidence.

personas.name: Language runtime owner
personas.goal: Run broad libc consumers such as Python-like runtimes with threads, dlopen, resolver, locale, malloc, stdio, and signal behavior intact.
personas.success_condition: Mode-pair failures identify the responsible subsystem and artifact bundle instead of collapsing into generic unsupported status.

personas.name: Threaded service operator
personas.goal: Run server workloads with stable threading, resolver, socket, allocator, signal, and performance behavior.
personas.success_condition: Threaded and performance-sensitive rows enforce strict/hardened behavior plus latency budget evidence before claims advance.

personas.name: Locale, iconv, and resolver user
personas.goal: Use name lookup, NSS, locale, collation, and encoding paths with deterministic compatibility diagnostics.
personas.success_condition: Resolver/NSS and locale/iconv rows have specific semantic-divergence signatures and user-facing next steps.

failure_taxonomy.user_impact: Program cannot start or load required symbols before main.
Program starts but fails when a required libc symbol is unresolved or advertised without support evidence.
representative_signatures: support matrix claim lacks fixture evidence

failure_taxonomy.user_impact: Program completes but stdout, stderr, errno, return value, file state, or timing semantics differ from host glibc contract.
Program corrupts, leaks, double-frees, or mixes ownership domains across host and FrankenLibC allocators.
Networking, identity lookup, package fetch, or service discovery fails or returns different answers.
Sort order, character classification, formatting, transliteration, or conversion differs from glibc.
Threaded workloads hang, race, deadlock, or diverge around cancellation and synchronization contracts.
Programs that rely on buffered IO, printf/scanf, FILE state, or stream errors observe glibc-incompatible behavior.
Interposition or safety membrane overhead makes otherwise correct workloads too slow for practical use.
A failure is detected but logs do not tell users or agents what to fix next.
README, release, or support artifacts advertise support that current evidence does not prove.

workloads.title: Interactive shell plus coreutils file and text commands

representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: Pinned argv, /tmp listing, and fixed locale C.UTF-8.

required_unit_tests: stdio formatting and flush boundary tests when stdio code changes
locale and ctype boundary tests when shell-visible locale code changes
process errno and wait status tests when process code changes

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: shell command fails before executing argv
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report loader phase, replacement level, runtime mode, and artifact bundle.

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: stdout, stderr, ferror, or buffered flush order differs
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report stream state, expected output, actual output, and FILE flags.

user_facing_diagnostics: show failed command and runtime mode
show nearest failing symbol family
show artifact bundle path
show whether standalone replacement claims are blocked

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: L2/L3 startup and host-dependency evidence is missing or stale
negative_claim_tests.evidence_probe: replacement_levels plus host dependency inventory freshness

workloads.title: Build tools compiling and linking small C/Rust projects

representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: Pinned smoke.c fixture, clean temp directory, fixed compiler argv.

required_unit_tests: allocator ownership and realloc edge tests when allocator code changes
process spawn and exit-status tests when process code changes
dlfcn and symbol version tests when ABI loader code changes

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: link or runtime loader reports missing symbol or unsupported version node
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report symbol, version, owning ABI module, and support evidence.

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: foreign free, realloc ownership, or allocator fingerprint failure
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report pointer class, generation, healing action, and mode.

user_facing_diagnostics: show compile, link, or run phase
show first missing or divergent symbol
show generated temp artifact bundle
show whether issue is toolchain, loader, or allocator owned

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: toolchain row still depends on host-glibc-backed startup or call-through evidence
negative_claim_tests.evidence_probe: replacement_levels L2 obligations plus host dependency inventory

workloads.title: Language runtime with threads, dlopen, resolver, locale, and stdio

representative_commands.command: python3 -c 'import ctypes, locale, socket, threading; print(locale.setlocale(locale.LC_CTYPE, "")); print(socket.getaddrinfo("localhost", 80)[0][0]); t=threading.Thread(target=lambda: None); t.start(); t.join()'
representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: localhost lookup, fixed locale environment, no network egress.

required_unit_tests: pthread lifecycle and cancellation tests when thread code changes
resolver localhost and invalid-name tests when resolver code changes
locale mb/wc boundary tests when locale or iconv code changes

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: thread lifecycle, mutex, condvar, or cancellation result differs
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report primitive, thread count, strict result, hardened result, and timeout.

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: localhost or NSS-backed lookup differs from host glibc
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report lookup name, source, expected records, actual records, and timeout.

user_facing_diagnostics: show failing imported module or subsystem
separate resolver, pthread, locale, dlfcn, and allocator failures
show strict versus hardened mode pair result
show artifact bundle and owner bead

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: thread, resolver, locale, or dlfcn hard-parts evidence is absent
negative_claim_tests.evidence_probe: hard-parts e2e catalog and mode-pair report freshness

workloads.title: Package-manager style metadata, fetch, verify, unpack, and install workflow

representative_commands.command: sh -lc 'mkdir -p pkgroot && printf metadata > pkgroot/METADATA && cat pkgroot/METADATA >/dev/null'
representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: Local-only package fixture; no network egress unless resolver row is explicitly selected.

required_unit_tests: filesystem errno and partial write tests when unistd/io code changes
resolver timeout and hosts-file tests when resolver code changes
time and file metadata boundary tests when time/resource code changes

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: metadata file state, errno, or process status differs
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report command phase, file path, expected state, actual state, and errno.

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: package source lookup differs or times out
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report lookup source, timeout policy, expected records, and actual records.

user_facing_diagnostics: show workflow phase: metadata, fetch, verify, unpack, install
show deterministic replay directory
show resolver and filesystem divergence separately
show claim block reason when only local fixture evidence exists

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: networked package fetch lacks resolver/NSS fixture evidence
negative_claim_tests.evidence_probe: resolver/NSS artifact references and e2e mode-pair logs

workloads.title: Threaded TCP-like service lifecycle

representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: Loopback-only socket workload with fixed request count and timeout.

required_unit_tests: mutex, condvar, rwlock, and TLS tests when pthread code changes
socket lifecycle and poll timeout tests when socket or poll code changes
thread-cache allocator tests when allocator code changes

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: service hangs, deadlocks, or mismatches strict/hardened thread outcome
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report primitive, timeout, thread count, and mode-pair classification.

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: service latency or contention budget exceeded
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report baseline, actual, budget, and hardware profile.

user_facing_diagnostics: show service phase and thread primitive
show strict/hardened mode pair result
show latency budget deltas
show whether failure is correctness or performance

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: threaded service mode-pair replay has not passed

workloads.title: Resolver and NSS identity/name lookup

representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: Pinned localhost and root lookups with captured host oracle fixtures.

required_unit_tests: getaddrinfo localhost and invalid host tests when resolver code changes
passwd/group positive and missing-record tests when NSS code changes
timeout and errno mapping tests for resolver failure paths

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: lookup status, canonical name, records, passwd entry, or errno mismatch
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report source, query, expected records, actual records, timeout, and errno.

user_facing_diagnostics: show query type and source
show expected versus actual record set
show timeout and errno mapping
show whether NSS backend is implemented, call-through, or unsupported

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: resolver/NSS row has no current host oracle fixture
negative_claim_tests.evidence_probe: fixture freshness and hard-parts dependency matrix

workloads.title: Locale, collation, multibyte, wide-char, and iconv conversion

representative_commands.command: locale-harness --locale C.UTF-8 --strcoll a b --iconv UTF-8 ISO-8859-1
representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: Pinned locale, encoding pair, and byte vectors.

required_unit_tests: setlocale positive, invalid, and default-locale tests when locale code changes
iconv valid, invalid, and partial-sequence tests when iconv code changes
wide-char boundary tests when wcsmbs code changes

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: collation, multibyte, wide-char, or conversion bytes differ
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report locale, encoding, input bytes, expected bytes, and actual bytes.

user_facing_diagnostics: show locale and encoding
show exact byte vector divergence
show fallback or unsupported semantic contract
show claim block reason for language-runtime rows

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: locale/iconv fixture coverage is missing for the advertised locale or encoding
negative_claim_tests.evidence_probe: semantic contract inventory and locale/iconv fixture refs

workloads.title: stdio/libio formatting, scanning, buffering, and stream state

representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: Pinned format strings, byte streams, and temporary file contents.

required_unit_tests: printf flags, width, precision, NaN, inf, and length-modifier tests when printf code changes
scanf tokenization and error recovery tests when scanner code changes
FILE state, buffering, ferror, and feof tests when stream code changes

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: printf, scanf, FILE state, buffering, or errno mismatch
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report format string, stream state, expected output, actual output, and flags.

user_facing_diagnostics: show format string or stream operation
show expected versus actual bytes
show ferror/feof and errno
show which user workflows are blocked by stdio/libio

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: stdio/libio native claim lacks stream-state fixture coverage
negative_claim_tests.evidence_probe: semantic contract inventory and stdio fixture refs

workloads.title: Allocator ownership, realloc, free, and membrane healing under stress

representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: Pinned allocation sequence, thread count, and invalid-pointer cases.

required_unit_tests: zero-size, max-size, align, realloc, and free tests when allocator code changes
foreign free, double free, quarantine, and generation tests when membrane allocator logic changes
thread-cache contention tests when allocator fast paths change

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: foreign free, double free, generation, or canary/fingerprint check diverges
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report pointer class, allocation metadata, healing action, and metric counters.

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: allocator or membrane latency exceeds strict or hardened budget
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report baseline, actual latency, budget, and contention profile.

user_facing_diagnostics: show allocation operation and pointer class
show strict versus hardened healing behavior
show membrane metric deltas
show latency budget impact

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: allocator row lacks invalid-pointer strict/hardened evidence
negative_claim_tests.evidence_probe: hardened repair/deny matrix and allocator oracle logs

workloads.title: Startup, dynamic linking, symbol versioning, TLS, and init/fini

representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: Pinned shared object fixtures and version script expectations.

required_unit_tests: version-script export tests when ABI maps change
TLS and init/fini order tests when startup code changes
dlopen/dlsym/dlclose edge tests when dlfcn code changes

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: loader, TLS, init/fini, or symbol version failure before user code
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report object, symbol, version, phase, and replacement level.

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: expected versioned ABI symbol is absent or incorrectly advertised
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report symbol, version node, support status, and fixture coverage.

user_facing_diagnostics: show failing loader phase
show symbol and version node
show whether host glibc is still required
show exact replacement-level claim that is blocked

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: custom rtld, CRT, TLS, and init/fini evidence is absent
negative_claim_tests.evidence_probe: replacement_levels L3 blockers and host dependency inventory

workloads.title: Performance-sensitive hot paths for string, malloc, stdio, pthread, syscall, and membrane

representative_commands.deterministic_inputs: Pinned benchmark families, iteration budget, and hardware metadata.

required_unit_tests: semantic parity tests before any hot-path optimization
strict and hardened membrane decision tests when validation fast paths change
regression tests for empty, boundary, and adversarial inputs after optimization

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: strict or hardened latency budget exceeded for a hot-path family
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report operation, baseline, actual, budget, hardware profile, and artifact refs.

failure_scenarios.expected_failure_signature: optimized path passes faster but breaks return, errno, output, or memory semantics
failure_scenarios.diagnostic_expectation: Report parity oracle, expected, actual, and optimized decision path.

user_facing_diagnostics: show benchmark family and mode
show strict and hardened budget deltas
show linked parity proof
show whether optimization is blocked by missing evidence

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: hot-path speedup lacks current parity proof and budget artifact
negative_claim_tests.evidence_probe: perf budget plus parity fixture artifacts

summary.claim_block_policy: Every workload row includes at least one negative claim test proving unsupported or missing-evidence cases remain claim_blocked.
